hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 pockets by mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) and introducing a polar/charged residue at position 6 to improve SSTR2 selectivity while preserving ddG.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity, but structural issues and stability limitations are reducing the pass rate. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions should improve yield without compromising selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures due to poor geometry.
Focus optimization on non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
